title: Outcome Measurement (Deprecated)
description:
**Deprecated as of this minor.** Predates the unified measurement-vocabulary pattern. Outcome metrics (incremental_sales_lift, brand_lift, foot_traffic, conversion_lift, brand_search_lift, attributed sales) are now expressible through the same primitives as the rest of the measurement surface:

- **Outcome metric capability** → declared via `reporting_capabilities.available_metrics` on the product. Closed enum entries: `incremental_sales_lift`, `brand_lift`, `foot_traffic`, `conversion_lift`, `brand_search_lift`. Existing entries `conversions`, `conversion_value`, `roas`, `cost_per_acquisition`, `units_sold`, `new_to_brand_rate`, `new_to_brand_units`, `leads` already covered most outcome-metric needs.
- **Attributed sales** (the retail-media value prop) → `conversion_value` with `qualifier.attribution_methodology: 'deterministic_purchase'`. **Walmart Connect's `attributedSalesIn14Days`** maps to `conversion_value` + `qualifier.attribution_methodology: 'deterministic_purchase'` + `qualifier.attribution_window: {interval: 14, unit: 'days'}`. Same pattern for Amazon DSP attributed sales, Kroger Precision attributed sales, Criteo Retail Media attributed sales — the protocol does not invent a separate `attributed_sales` metric ID because the qualifier handles the methodology split.
- **Brand lift** → `brand_lift` metric_id PLUS `qualifier.lift_dimension` (closed enum: awareness, consideration, favorability, purchase_intent, ad_recall). Brand-lift vendors (Kantar, Upwave, Cint, DV) report each dimension separately; the qualifier ensures rows aren't combined.
- **Attribution methodology** → `qualifier.attribution_methodology` (`deterministic_purchase`, `probabilistic`, `panel_based`, `modeled`).
- **Attribution window** → `qualifier.attribution_window` (structured duration `{interval, unit}`, never a shorthand string).
- **Vendor identity** (typically the seller for retail-media closed loop) → vendor-scope `committed_metrics` entries with the BrandRef anchored on the seller's measurement-agent capability (or absent when the seller is the implicit vendor). For third-party retail measurement (Circana, NielsenIQ, IRI for retail MMM; Kantar/Upwave for brand lift on retail-media inventory), use `performance_standards.vendor` to pin the third-party identity.
- **Reporting frequency / format** → planned for a follow-up `reporting_capabilities` extension; existing `outcome_measurement.reporting` field continues to work for one minor.

This schema is retained for one-minor backwards compatibility on `product.outcome_measurement`. Removed at the next major. New implementations SHOULD declare outcome capabilities via `reporting_capabilities.available_metrics` and consumers reconcile via `committed_metrics`/`metric_aggregates` with the appropriate qualifier.

window.description: Attribution window as a structured duration (e.g., {"interval": 30, "unit": "days"}).
